topics: Binary, Octal, and Hexadecimal Number Systems - Representation of Negative Numbers in Binary - Binary Arithmetic
Error Detection and Correction Codes - Parity & Hamming Codes
De Morgan's Theorems - SOP & POS Boolean Expressions and Truth Tables - Formation of Switching Functions from Word Statements
Minimization of Boolean Expressions - Algebraic, Karnaugh Map (Up to 5 Variables) - Realization Using Basic Gates and Universal Gates
Adders & Subtractors - Types, Ripple Carry & Carry Look Ahead Adders, BCD Adder
Basics of Verilog Programming - Basic Language Elements (Identifiers, Data Objects, Scalar Data Types, Operators)
Modelling and Implementation of Logic Gates Using Verilog Codes
Modelling and Implementation of Simple Combinational Circuits at the Gate Level Using Verilog Codes
Flip-Flops - SR, D & T Flip Flops - Level & Edge Triggered Flip Flops - Synchronous & Asynchronous Inputs - Conversion Between Flip Flops - Master Slave Flip Flops
Shift Registers - SISO, SIPO, PISO, PIPO
Counters - Asynchronous Counters (Up, Down, Up/Down), Mod N Counters
Shift Register Counters - Ring & Johnson Counters
Introduction to Design of Synchronous Sequential Circuits Using Finite State Machines - Mealy & Moore Types - Flip Flops
Logic Families - Introduction to Different Logic Families
Standard Logic Levels - Current and Voltage Parameters - Fan In and Fan Out - Propagation Delay - Noise Consideration
TTL - Basic Working Principle of a TTL NAND Gate - Totem Pole and Open Collector Gate Output Configurations - Tri-State Logic - Characteristics of a TTL NAND Gate
CMOS - Basic Working Principle of a CMOS Inverter
